# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the base directory for templates
define('TEMPLATES_DIR', __DIR__ . '/templates/');

// --- Configuration ---
// Define allowed pages to prevent directory traversal and arbitrary file inclusion.
// This is a critical security measure.
$allowedPages = [
    'home' => 'home.php',
    'about' => 'about.php',
    'contact' => 'contact.php',
    'services' => 'services.php',
    'privacy-policy' => 'privacy-policy.php',
    // Add more allowed pages as your site grows
];

// --- Router Logic ---

// Get the requested page from the URL parameter.
// Use 'home' as the default page if no 'page' parameter is provided.
$requestedPageKey = $_GET['page'] ?? 'home';

// Check if the requested page is in the list of allowed pages.
if (array_key_exists($requestedPageKey, $allowedPages)) {
    $templateFileName = $allowedPages[$requestedPageKey];
    $templatePath = TEMPLATES_DIR . $templateFileName;

    // Verify that the file actually exists on the disk.
    // This adds another layer of security and prevents errors if a file is missing.
    if (file_exists($templatePath)) {
        // Include the template file.
        // All variables defined before this include will be available inside the template.
        include $templatePath;
    } else {
        // Handle case where the allowed page file does not exist (e.g., deleted or misconfigured).
        // You might want to log this error.
        header("HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found");
        include TEMPLATES_DIR . '404.php'; // Include a generic 404 template
    }
} else {
    // Handle unauthorized or non-existent page requests.
    // This is typically a 404 Not Found error.
    header("HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found");
    include TEMPLATES_DIR . '404.php'; // Include a generic 404 template
}

?>

<!-- Example Usage (How to link to pages) -->
<!--
<nav>
    <a href="?page=home">Home</a>
    <a href="?page=about">About Us</a>
    <a href="?page=contact">Contact</a>
    <a href="?page=services">Services</a>
    <a href="?page=privacy-policy">Privacy Policy</a>
</nav>
-->

<!--
  Directory Structure Example:

  /
  ├── index.php         (This router file)
  └── templates/
      ├── home.php
      ├── about.php
      ├── contact.php
      ├── services.php
      ├── privacy-policy.php
      └── 404.php         (Error page template)

  Example template content for home.php:
  ---------------------------------------
  <h1>Welcome to the Home Page!</h1>
  <p>This is the content for the home page.</p>
  ---------------------------------------

  Example template content for 404.php:
  ---------------------------------------
  <h1>404 Not Found</h1>
  <p>Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist.</p>
  ---------------------------------------
-->
